title: Brent Spence Bridge Corridor - Major Announcements (2010-2024)
text:
Brent Spence Bridge Corridor - Major Announcements (2010-2024)









Date
Event
Type
Description




2012-10-01
Original FONSI Approved
Planning
FHWA approves the original environmental impact study (Alternative I).


2020-11-11
Bridge Fire & Shutdown
Incident
Truck fire shuts down bridge for 6 weeks, sparking national urgency.


2022-02-28
DeWine/Beshear MOU
Funding
Governors sign MOU to seek federal funding and build without tolls.


2022-12-29
$1.6B Federal Grant
Funding
Biden administration awards largest-ever federal grant to the project.


2023-01-04
Presidential Visit
Political
Biden, McConnell, and Governors visit bridge to celebrate funding.


2023-07-27
Contractor Award
Execution
Walsh Kokosing awarded design-build contract.


2024-05-10
Final Federal Clearing
Planning
FHWA issues final Supplemental FONSI, clearing path for construction.


2024-05-31
Design Innovations
Design
Official announcement of new street grid and reduced park impacts.

title: Event Study: Brent Spence Bridge Corridor Impact
text: This analysis tracks home price evolution near the Brent Spence Bridge (BSB) corridor in response to major project milestones. We find a significant “landing zone” effect: ZIP codes within 1 mile of the project (e.g., 45203, 41014) have outperformed the broader region by 8.5 percentage points since the 2020 fire.

title: Event Study: Brent Spence Bridge Corridor Impact
text: This analysis tracks home price evolution near the Brent Spence Bridge (BSB) corridor in response to major project milestones. We find a significant “landing zone” effect: ZIP codes within 1 mile of the project (e.g., 45203, 41014) have outperformed the broader region by 8.5 percentage points since the 2020 fire.

title: Event Study: Brent Spence Bridge Corridor Impact
text:
Event Analysis
We analyzed four major “shocks” to the project timeline. For each event, we normalized prices to the month of the announcement (Index = 100).

1. The $1.6B Federal Grant (Dec 2022)
This was the single largest funding commitment in the project’s history.


Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0.
i Please use `linewidth` instead.











2. Contractor Award (July 2023)
The award to Walsh Kokosing signaled that the project was moving from “political talk” to “physical execution.”

title: Event Study: Brent Spence Bridge Corridor Impact
text:
Neighborhood Focus: The “Landing Zone”
The most impacted ZIP codes are 45203 (Queensgate/West End) and 41014 (West Covington). These areas are directly adjacent to the new bridge landings and have seen the most intense speculative activity.
